title: Peer Pressure Purchase
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you maintain friendships while staying within your financial limits?
What strategies help you confidently say no to spending that doesn't align with your goals?

stages.prompt: Friends push you to spend beyond comfort. What's best?

options.label: Follow them
options.reflection: Following friends beyond your comfort zone can lead to financial stress and spending that doesn't align with your personal goals.

options.label: Stick to your limits
options.reflection: Exactly! Sticking to your financial limits shows financial maturity and helps maintain your financial health regardless of peer pressure.

options.label: Sometimes follow, sometimes say no
options.reflection: While flexibility has merit, consistently adhering to your financial limits is more important for maintaining financial health.

options.label: Spend even more to show I can afford it
options.reflection: Trying to outspend friends to prove financial capability often leads to financial strain and unnecessary competition.

stages.prompt: How does peer pressure affect your financial decisions?

options.label: It can push you to make unwise spending choices
options.reflection: Exactly! Peer pressure can lead to unwise spending choices that don't align with your financial goals or budget.

options.label: It improves your financial decision-making
options.reflection: Peer pressure typically doesn't improve financial decision-making as it often encourages spending beyond comfort zones.

options.label: It motivates better financial habits
options.reflection: Peer pressure usually motivates spending to match others rather than developing better financial habits.

options.label: It inspires more responsible spending
options.reflection: Peer pressure usually inspires competitive spending rather than more responsible financial behavior.

stages.prompt: What's the wisest way to handle group spending activities?

options.label: Match what others are spending
options.reflection: Matching others' spending can lead to financial stress if their budgets are different from yours.

options.label: Plan ahead and stick to your budget
options.reflection: Perfect! Planning ahead and sticking to your budget allows you to participate while maintaining financial control.

options.label: Compete to show financial capability
options.reflection: Competing financially with others often leads to unnecessary spending and financial stress.

options.label: Avoid all group spending activities
options.reflection: Avoiding all group activities might protect finances but could limit social connections. Planning ahead is a better balance.

stages.prompt: How can you maintain friendships while managing peer pressure?

options.label: Agree to all spending suggestions to maintain harmony
options.reflection: Always agreeing to spending suggestions can harm your finances and may not create genuine respect in friendships.

options.label: Hide your financial limitations from friends
options.reflection: Hiding financial limitations can lead to situations where you're forced to spend beyond your means.

options.label: Financial confidence includes saying no
options.reflection: Exactly! Having financial confidence to say no when needed helps maintain both your finances and your self-respect in relationships.

options.label: Judge friends who spend differently than you
options.reflection: Judging friends' spending habits won't help with peer pressure and may damage relationships.

stages.prompt: What strategy helps resist peer pressure to overspend?

options.label: Blame others for your spending decisions
options.reflection: Blaming others for your spending decisions removes your personal financial responsibility and doesn't address peer pressure.

options.label: Ignore your financial limits temporarily
options.reflection: Ignoring financial limits, even temporarily, can lead to financial stress and doesn't build lasting financial confidence.

options.label: Justify overspending as relationship investments
options.reflection: Consistently justifying overspending as relationship investments can lead to financial stress and unsustainable habits.

options.label: Prepare responses for social spending situations
options.reflection: Perfect! Preparing responses helps you stick to your financial limits while maintaining relationships and confidence.
